items.claim: Conor McGregor has not shown himself to be chinny despite his leg injury.
items.quote: Conor broke his leg, but he hasn't really shown us that he's chinny or something

items.claim: Max Holloway lacks significant knockout power.
items.quote: Max Holloway doesn't have a lot of power.

items.claim: Conor McGregor has become a true welterweight and is significantly larger than in his first fight with Max Holloway.
items.quote: Conor is a true welterweight now. Conor's a lot bigger than he was when he first fought Max Holloway

items.claim: Conor McGregor's current boxing coach has negatively impacted his performance by focusing on sitting down on his punches.
items.quote: And And one of the issues, I believe, is this Crumlin boxing coach. There's the There's this old man. And since he's emerged, McGregor's gotten significantly worse.

items.claim: Conor McGregor has become slower and lost his snappy timing due to a focus on heavy-handed punching mechanics.
items.quote: Now that Conor's heavier on his feet, he's slower. His biggest thing was his timing, his speed, his power. All that's All that's been diminished just for raw heaviness.

items.claim: Conor McGregor's history of substance abuse and alcohol addiction acts as a significant hurdle for his return to five-round fights.
items.quote: That's a lot for a guy who's getting over a drug and alcohol addiction, you know?

items.claim: Conor McGregor has been inactive in the cage for five years.
items.quote: Conor McGregor, after not one, not two, not three, NOT FOUR, BUT FIVE years out of the cage, is going to step back into the UFC's octagon
